title: Imagined Speech EEG dataset comparing paradigm designs (Aguilera-Rodriguez et al. 2025)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 15 healthy participants performing imagined speech tasks across four directional commands (avanzar, retroceder, derecha, izquierda). The study compares two experimental paradigm designs: a traditional cue-based approach with visual and auditory cues, and a gamified Pac-Man maze-based paradigm. Data were acquired at 500 Hz using 24 EEG channels and include 1,800 trials (120 per subject across 2 sessions) with balanced class distribution, providing a resource for developing and benchmarking brain-computer interface systems based on imagined speech.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded from 15 healthy participants (8 male, 7 female, ages 18-27) using a mBrainTrain Smarting system with 24 channels sampled at 500 Hz. Two experimental paradigms were employed: (1) Traditional cue-based paradigm with visual and auditory cues (5 beeps at 1.4s rhythm), followed by 7 repetitions of imagined speech with the last 3 extracted for analysis and 2s inter-trial rest; (2) Gamified paradigm using a Pac-Man maze interface with four sequential steps (movement decision, imagined speech, vocalized speech, character movement), each lasting 1.4s. Participants performed 120 trials per session across 2 sessions, with equal distribution across four imagined speech classes. Data were recorded with FCz reference and Fpz ground, with 60 Hz line frequency notch.
